description: The set of accounts that may be used for billing for this Encounter.
The class history permits the tracking of the encounters transitions without needing to go through the entity history.
The list of diagnosis relevant to this encounter.
Where a specific encounter should be classified as a part of a specific episode(s) of care this field should be used.
Any special requests that have been made for this hospitalization encounter, such as the provision of specific equipment or other things.
Special courtesies (VIP, board member).
Used to track patient's diet restrictions and/or preference.
List of locations where the patient has been during this encounter.
The list of people responsible for providing the service.
The participant type indicates how an individual participates in an encounter.
Reason the encounter takes place, expressed as a code. For admissions, this can be used for a coded admission diagnosis.
The referral request this encounter satisfies (incoming referral).
The status history permits the encounter entity to contain the status history without needing to read through the historical versions of the entity, or even have the server store them
Specific type of encounter (e.g. e-mail consultation, surgical day-care, skilled nursing, rehabilitation).
The set of accounts that may be used for billing for this Episode Of Care.
The list of practitioners that may be facilitating this episode of care for specific purposes.
The list of diagnosis relevant to this episode of care.
The history of statuses that the Episode Of Care has been through (without requiring processing the history of the resource).
Referral Request(s) that are fulfilled by this Episode Of Care, incoming referrals.
A classification of the type of episode of care; e.g. specialist referral, disease management, type of funded care.
